criteria_verification.evidence: src/jarvis/tools/general.py defines `get_calendar_events` decorated with `@tool(parse_docstring=True)` and exports it via `__all__`. The langchain StructuredTool exposes the call surface `get_calendar_events(window="today") -> str` (verified via .invoke({}) returning '[]' and .name == 'get_calendar_events'). TestAC001ToolExposed in tests/test_get_calendar_events.py covers exposure, name, BaseTool type, default-window behaviour, and the str return annotation.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Docstring matches API-tools.md §1.3 byte-for-byte; argument type annotation is `Literal["today","tomorrow","this_week"]`.
criteria_verification.evidence: The function's window parameter is annotated `Literal["today", "tomorrow", "this_week"] = "today"`, and a module-level `CalendarWindow = Literal["today", "tomorrow", "this_week"]` alias is exported. The docstring is lifted verbatim from docs/design/FEAT-JARVIS-002/contracts/API-tools.md §1.3. TestAC002DocstringAndAnnotation::test_docstring_matches_api_tools_md_section_1_3_byte_for_byte parses the contract markdown and asserts the function's __doc__ matches the contract docstring after stripping whitespace; the typing.get_type_hints assertion confirms the Literal annotation.

TestAC002DocstringAndAnnotation::test_docstring_matches_api_tools_md_section_1_3_byte_for_byte parses the contract markdown and asserts the function's __doc__ matches the contract docstring after stripping whitespace; the typing.get_type_hints assertion confirms the Literal annotation.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Returns JSON `"[]"` (Phase 2 stub) for any valid `window`.
criteria_verification.evidence: All three valid windows (today, tomorrow, this_week) return the string '[]' via both .invoke({"window": ...}) and the underlying .func(...) entry points. TestAC003ValidWindowReturnsEmptyJsonList covers each window with parametrize.
criteria_verification.notes: All three valid windows (today, tomorrow, this_week) return the string '[]' via both .invoke({"window": ...}) and the underlying .func(...) entry points. TestAC003ValidWindowReturnsEmptyJsonList covers each window with parametrize.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Rejects invalid window with `ERROR: invalid_window — must be one of today/tomorrow/this_week, got <value>` listing the allowed windows.
criteria_verification.evidence: When the runtime guard sees a window value outside _ALLOWED_WINDOWS the function returns 'ERROR: invalid_window — must be one of today/tomorrow/this_week, got <value>' verbatim, with the bad value echoed back. TestAC004InvalidWindowReturnsStructuredError parametrizes six bad values (next_year, yesterday, '', TODAY, this-week, 'today ') and asserts both the exact prefix and the trailing got-<value> echo, plus a separate test that asserts each allowed window appears in the message.
criteria_verification.notes: When the runtime guard sees a window value outside _ALLOWED_WINDOWS the function returns 'ERROR: invalid_window — must be one of today/tomorrow/this_week, got <value>' verbatim, with the bad value echoed back. TestAC004InvalidWindowReturnsStructuredError parametrizes six bad values (next_year, yesterday, '', TODAY, this-week, 'today ') and asserts both the exact prefix and the trailing got-<value> echo, plus a separate test that asserts each allowed window appears in the message.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Returned shape is a JSON array of `CalendarEvent`-shaped dicts (even when empty) so FEAT-JARVIS-007's morning-briefing skill parses identically against stub and real data.
criteria_verification.evidence: The Phase 2 stub returns `json.dumps([])` so json.loads(result) yields a Python list (zero-length but still an array). TestAC005JsonArrayShape verifies json.loads round-trips to a list and that the canonical literal '[]' is returned. The stable Literal-driven schema and the unchanged docstring contract guarantee the shape will not drift when the real provider lands in v1.5.
criteria_verification.notes: The Phase 2 stub returns `json.dumps([])` so json.loads(result) yields a Python list (zero-length but still an array). TestAC005JsonArrayShape verifies json.loads round-trips to a list and that the canonical literal '[]' is returned. The stable Literal-driven schema and the unchanged docstring contract guarantee the shape will not drift when the real provider lands in v1.5.

criteria_verification.evidence: The function body is wrapped in a try/except catch-all per ADR-ARCH-021. The Literal annotation defends against schema-bound bad input via pydantic, and the runtime guard plus catch-all defend against direct Python callers. TestAC006NeverRaises feeds pathological inputs (empty string, embedded null, shell-injection text, multi-byte unicode, non-string None) through .func and asserts a string starting with 'ERROR: invalid_window —' is always returned, never an exception.
